articles.title: Booking a counseling intake — what to expect at your first session
articles.content: Schedule a counseling intake online or by phone. Your first session is a confidential conversation about what brings you in, your history, and your goals; together you and the counselor decide next steps, which may include short-term therapy, a group, or a referral. There is no cost for the initial sessions. Counseling is separate from academics and your records are private.
articles.snippet: Schedule a counseling intake online or by phone

articles.title: Same-day urgent appointments and after-hours crisis support
articles.content: If you are in crisis during business hours, walk in or call Counseling Services and ask for an urgent same-day appointment. After hours, call or text the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline, or call campus safety or 911 if you are in immediate danger. You never have to be in danger to reach out — if something feels like too much, we want to talk with you today.
articles.snippet: If you are in crisis during business hours, walk in or call Counseling Services and ask for an urgent same-day appointment

articles.title: CAPS group therapy and peer-support communities
articles.content: Counseling & Psychological Services (CAPS) runs therapy and support groups on themes like anxiety, grief, identity, and adjusting to college. Peer-support communities, led by trained students, offer a lower-barrier place to feel less alone. Groups are confidential and free; join through an intake or by contacting CAPS directly.
articles.snippet: Counseling & Psychological Services (CAPS) runs therapy and support groups on themes like anxiety, grief, identity, and adjusting to college

articles.title: Basic needs: food pantry, emergency funds, housing insecurity support
articles.content: No student should have to choose between books and meals. The campus food pantry is free and confidential, emergency micro-grants can help with an unexpected bill, and case managers assist students facing housing insecurity. Reach out early — we can connect you to campus and community resources and, when relevant, to Financial Aid for a special-circumstances review.
articles.snippet: No student should have to choose between books and meals

articles.title: Accessibility services and academic accommodations
articles.content: Students with disabilities — including chronic health, mental-health, and learning conditions — can register with Accessibility Services to arrange accommodations such as extended testing time, note-taking support, or housing adjustments. Bring documentation if you have it; if not, we can help you get it. Accommodations are confidential and arranged collaboratively with you and your instructors.
articles.snippet: Students with disabilities — including chronic health, mental-health, and learning conditions — can register with Accessibility Services to arrange accommoda...

articles.title: Substance use support — confidential and non-disciplinary pathways
articles.content: Support for alcohol and other drug concerns is confidential and separate from the conduct process. You can meet one-on-one with a wellness counselor, join a recovery-ally community, or get a referral for higher-level care. Seeking help on your own will not trigger discipline; our goal is your health and safety.
articles.snippet: Support for alcohol and other drug concerns is confidential and separate from the conduct process
